Validated against the live Midway widget. The popup search uses input#search-field inside .midway-search-input-container and calls GET https://shop.fassride.com/Products/Search?search={sku}. Use the JSON products[] hit, score by partNumber, then build https://www.fassride.com/details?id={id}. The raw search endpoint alone is not sufficient for prefixed alias SKUs because it can return fuzzy matches.

Validated against the live Partslogic storefront. The homepage widget uses input#sui-search-bar-input inside #pl-search-bar-container, but the stronger runtime path is direct GET /search.html?q={sku}. That results page returns /i-... product links for queries like 67OP, so treat it as a results-page clickthrough resolver rather than a popup-only search.
Known results-page search route; direct fetch is currently blocked here by Cloudflare 403, but first result is typically the target product.

Validated from https://www.royaltycore.com/search-2.html. Site exposes a GET form action https://www.royaltycore.com/search.html with input name q, producing results URLs like https://www.royaltycore.com/search.html?q={sku}.
